category_title: Eagerly participating students who raise hands and respond during group vocabulary lessons
minimal_context: The student is sitting with peers in a small table group and is expected to write about a picture on a worksheet.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Here is the worksheet. Please look at the picture and write about it.

STUDENT: (Sits quietly at the table group, looking at the worksheet but not picking up the iPad to take notes.)

TUTOR: Go ahead and start writing.

STUDENT: (Remains passively engaged, watching the other classmates in the small group instead of working.)

TUTOR: Keep trying, you need to fill in your responses.

STUDENT: (Continues to show passive engagement, sitting silently without using the iPad or label maker to fill in responses on the worksheet.)

options.A: Immediately remove all demands and task materials from the table group for 60 seconds.
options.B: Teach the technique of "stepping back" and not acting on the "first thought" during the task.
options.C: Provide prompting and coaching to help the student begin the writing task.
options.D: Provide the possibility for the student to take micro-breaks from the task if needed.
